mechanism_narrative: A steady increase in nonfarm payroll employment indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months following payroll increases, or rank IC < 0.05 over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE > 5.0% is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances over a 3-year window, or average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation (month-over-month increase > 0.5%) and a high federal funds rate (>5.0%) indicates that the Fed is behind the curve, forcing more aggressive tightening that reduces present value of future cash flows and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: If the combination yields positive 20-day returns in more than 30% of occurrences over a 5-year walk-forward period, or average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime (UNRATE below 4.5%), an increase in housing starts signals that the economy is overheating and may prompt monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. Conversely, in high-unemployment regimes, the same signal is positive for equities, but this hypothesis targets the low-unemployment regime only.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE is below 4.5%, a one-standard deviation increase in HOUST is associated with positive average 20-day returns, or rank IC for HOUST conditional on UNRATE < 4.5% is positive over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals robust manufacturing activity and positive economic momentum, which is quickly priced into equities over the following week, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO MoM > 0.5% is followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 2-year period, or average return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,500 thousand units (annualized rate), it indicates residential construction is above the long-term trend and may lead to oversupply and a subsequent slowdown in GDP, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1500, forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over a 5-year window, or average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation is low (CPIAUCSL year-over-year increase below 2%), an increase in industrial production signals supply-side growth that boosts corporate earnings without triggering inflation fears, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days. In high inflation regimes the signal would be negative, but this hypothesis focuses on the low inflation regime.
failure_condition: In periods when CPIAUCSL YoY is below 2%, a one-standard deviation increase in INDPRO is associated with negative average 20-day returns, or rank IC for INDPRO is negative over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.25%, it indicates that monetary policy is sufficiently restrictive to slow economic activity and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 5.25% is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances over a 5-year period, or average return is positive.
